data.body: The /dso:onboarding skill asks the user to review the agent's understanding of the project (e.g. confirming detected frameworks, test commands, architecture), but then writes output artifacts (project-understanding.md, dso-config.conf, CLAUDE.md updates, etc.) without giving the user a chance to review how that understanding is being applied. The user should be able to see and approve the generated artifacts before they are written to disk. The understanding may be correct but the resulting config or documentation could misrepresent it, contain unwanted defaults, or omit important nuance. Fix: after the Socratic dialogue and before writing any files, present the planned artifacts (or a summary of what will be written and where) for user approval.
